title: Creative Policy
description: Creative requirements and restrictions for a product

provenance_required.description: Whether creatives must include provenance metadata. When true, the seller requires buyers to attach provenance declarations to creative submissions. The seller may independently verify claims via get_creative_features.

provenance_requirements.description: Structured provenance requirements for creatives. Refines provenance_required: when provenance_required is true, the fields in this object specify which provenance features the seller requires. When provenance_required is false or absent, this object SHOULD be absent; if present, receivers SHOULD ignore it. Existing seller agents that do not read this object are unaffected; the wire shape does not change for them. This is a visibility surface, not a prevention surface: provenance_requirements declares what the seller expects, but does not block delivery if requirements are unmet. A non-enforcing seller silently accepts non-compliant creative. The should-verify tier (receiver-obligation RFC) is intended to close this enforcement gap by adding task-level error signals that make unmet requirements visible to the buyer.

require_digital_source_type.description: When true, the seller requires creatives to include a digital_source_type field in their provenance, set to a valid value from the digital-source-type enum (not null or absent). Supports EU AI Act Art. 50 and CA SB 942 compliance workflows where AI disclosure metadata must be present at the protocol level.

require_disclosure_metadata.description: When true, the seller requires creatives to include a disclosure object in their provenance with disclosure.required set to a boolean value (true or false). When disclosure.required is true, at least one entry in disclosure.jurisdictions is expected.

require_embedded_provenance.description: When true, the seller requires creatives to include at least one embedded_provenance entry. For pipelines where sidecar metadata is stripped by intermediaries, this ensures provenance data persists through delivery.
